title: Braided rope structure
abstract: A braided rope structure includes a first segment and a second segment. The first segment has a first end and a second end opposite the first end. The first segment includes first strands braided into a first braid pattern. The second segment has a third end coupled to the second end. The second segment has the first strands braided into a second braid pattern different than the first braid pattern. The first strands extend continuously along the first segment and the second segment.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A braided rope structure comprising: a first segment having a first end and a second end opposite the first end, the first segment including strands braided into a first braid pattern; and a second segment having a third end coupled to the second end, the second segment having a number of the strands braided into a second braid pattern different than the first braid pattern, wherein the strands extend continuously along the first segment and the second segment. 2. The braided rope structure of claim 1, wherein the first segment includes a body, and wherein the second segment includes a number of legs furcated from the body, each leg including a number of the strands braided into the second braid pattern, a total number of the strands in the legs being equal to a total number of the strands in the first segment. 3. The braided rope structure of claim 2, wherein the second segment includes two legs. 4. The braided rope structure of claim 3, wherein each leg includes the same number of the strands. 5. The braided rope structure of claim 3, wherein the legs include different numbers of the strands. 6. The braided rope structure of claim 3, wherein the braided rope structure further comprises a third segment having a fourth end coupled to the first end, the third segment having a number of the strands braided into the second braid pattern, the third segment including a number of legs furcated from the body, each leg including a number of the strands braided into the second braid pattern, a total number of the strands in the legs of the third segment being equal to a total number of the strands in the first segment. 7. The braided rope structure of claim 6, wherein the second segment and third segment each have two legs. 8. The braided rope structure of any preceding claim, wherein the second segment further includes a fifth end opposite the third end, and wherein the braided rope structure further comprises a fourth segment having a sixth end coupled to the fifth end, the fourth segment including strands braided into the first braid pattern. 9. The braided rope structure of claim 8, wherein the legs of the second segment are spaced apart, and wherein the braided rope structure further comprises an eye between the first segment and the fourth segment. 10. The braided rope structure of claim 9, further comprising a knot coupled to the fourth segment, the knot being removably positionable through the eye. 11. The braided rope structure of any preceding claim, further comprising an axial member extending generally parallel to the first segment and the second segment. 12. The braided rope structure of claim 11, wherein the axial member is a core, and wherein the first segment and the second segment are interwoven around the core. 13. The braided rope structure of claim 12, wherein the core includes a metallic material. 14. The braided rope structure of claim 11, wherein the axial member is interwoven into the first segment and the second segment. 15. The braided rope structure of claim 11, wherein the axial member extends away from the first segment, an opening being formed between the axial member and the first segment. 16. The braided rope structure of any preceding claim, wherein a length of the first segment is at least about four times a diameter of the first segment. 17. The braided rope structure of any preceding claim, wherein the first segment and the second segment have a total length, a length of the first segment being at least half the total length. 18. The braided rope structure of any preceding claim, wherein the first segment includes at least twenty-four strands. 19. A braided rope structure comprising: a first segment including strands braided into a first braid pattern; a second segment furcated from the first segment, the second segment including a number of the strands braided into a second braid pattern different than the first braid pattern; and a third segment furcated from the first segment in a different direction than the second segment, the third segment including a number of the strands braided into a third braid pattern different than the first braid pattern; wherein the strands extend and are braided continuously along the first segment, the second segment, and the third segment. 20. The braided rope structure of claim 19, wherein the first segment has an elongated body with a first furcation point and a second furcation point spaced apart from the first furcation point, the second segment extending from the first furcation point and the third segment extending from the second furcation point. 21. The braided rope structure of claim 20, wherein the first segment, the second segment, and the third segment have a total length, a length of the first segment being at least one-third of the total length. 22. The braided rope structure of any of claims 19-21, wherein the second segment has a first number of legs furcated from the first segment, and wherein the third segment has a second number of legs furcated from the first segment. 23. The braided rope structure of claim 22, wherein the first segment, the second segment, and the third segment make up a first unit, wherein the braided rope structure further comprises a second unit substantially identical to the first unit, a leg of the second segment of the first unit being coupled to a leg of the second segment of the second unit. 24. The braided rope structure of claim 23, wherein the braided rope structure further comprises an eye between the leg of the second segment of the first unit coupled to the leg of the second segment of the second unit. 25. The braided rope structure of claim 24, wherein the first unit extends in a first axial direction, and wherein the second unit extends in a second axial direction parallel to and offset from the first axial direction. 26. The braided rope structure of claim 24, wherein the first unit extends in a first axial direction, and wherein the second unit extends in a second axial direction parallel to and generally coaxial with the first axial direction. 27. The braided rope structure of claim 19, wherein the first segment includes a furcation point, and wherein the second segment and the third segment both extend from the furcation point. 28. The braided rope structure of claim 27, whereon the number of stands of the second segment are equal to the number of strands in the third segment. 29. The braided rope structure of claim 27 or 28, further comprising: a fourth segment furcated from the third segment, the fourth segment including a number of the strands braided into a fourth braid pattern different than the third braid pattern; and a fifth segment furcated from the third segment in a different direction than the fourth segment, the fifth segment including a number of the strands braided into a fifth braid pattern different than the third braid pattern. 30. The braided rope structure of any of claims 19-29, wherein the first segment includes at least twenty-four strands. 31. The braided rope structure of any of claims 19-30, further comprising an axial member extending generally parallel to the first segment and the second segment. 32. A braided rope structure comprising: strands braided into a first braid pattern along a first segment; a number of the strands furcated from the first segment and braided into a second braid pattern along a second segment, the second braid pattern being different than the first braid pattern; and a number of strands furcated from the first segment and braided into a third braid pattern along a third segment, the third braid pattern being different than the first braid pattern; wherein the second segment and the third segment furcate from a common point.
